human_verification.notes: Verified customs analytics boundary handling, research source-class coverage, metric-definition and reconciliation controls, unsupported-claim refusal, and scenario-to-ability mapping completeness.

review_notes: All 12 scenarios pass with explicit metric-definition, reconciliation, unsupported-claim, and escalation behavior.
Required shared, analytics-reporting-and-disclosure, and financial-services-and-insurance abilities are fully covered and mapped.

specialist_owned_evidence: Metric-definition logic is explicit and tied to named customs and financial source records.
Conflicting, stale, or incomplete source data is escalated instead of normalized into unsupported reporting.
Fraud, AML, consumer-impact, and legal-interpretation boundaries route to adjacent owners with structured evidence.
Runtime remains `specification-based`, with no retained-implementation claim embedded in this package.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-U.S. jurisdiction scope is captured as tenant-supplied runtime context rather than guessed in-pack.
Pack is ready for validated specification-based deployment with explicit promotion criteria for a future retained-implementation package.
